business_description.nature_of_business: Commercial construction contractor performing carpentry, electrical wiring, plumbing, masonry, interior painting, project management, and clerical operations

coverages_discussed.notes: Policy WC-KCB-2025-5580; PCRB Large Risk Rating Plan; Risk ID 491498-1604; Experience Modification Factor 0.86 (14% credit); total payroll $31,594,850 across 7 classifications; interstate rating: No

positives: Favorable experience modification factor of 0.86, representing a 14% premium credit below unity
Calculated mod (0.86) aligns exactly with the final applied modification, indicating no manual override or exception
Lowest claim count year (2022-2023) had only 3 claims with total incurred of $116,479
Qualifies for Large Risk Rating Plan, indicating significant premium volume and credibility
Clerical class (8810) carries low expected loss rate of 0.195 per $100 payroll
Total actual incurred losses ($548,404 combined) are well below total expected losses ($1,095,520)
concerns: Highest claim year (2021-2022) had 10 claims with total incurred of $240,694
Combined claim count of 20 across three policy years reflects elevated frequency for a construction risk
Excess losses of $212,733 represent approximately 39% of total actual losses, suggesting some severity exposure
High-hazard classifications (5022 Masonry, 5403 Carpentry) represent the two largest expected loss segments at $318,521 and $307,580 respectively
Expected Losses used in rating calculation ($1,095,520) exceed the payroll/classification data subtotal ($951,902), indicating a data discrepancy between worksheet sections
Expected Excess Losses ($1,132,496) substantially exceed total expected losses ($1,095,520), warranting review of excess loss factor inputs
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Three-year loss history (2021-2022 through 2023-2024): 20 total claims; combined total incurred $548,404; combined primary losses $335,671; combined excess losses $212,733. Year-by-year detail: 2023-2024 — 7 claims, total incurred $191,231, primary $120,455, excess $70,776; 2022-2023 — 3 claims, total incurred $116,479, primary $58,500, excess $57,979; 2021-2022 — 10 claims, total incurred $240,694, primary $156,716, excess $83,978.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: PCRB Experience Rating Worksheet prepared by Hallmark Insurance Group for Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. under policy WC-KCB-2025-5580. Valuation date 2026-03-15. Final Experience Modification Factor: 0.86. Rating Organization: Pennsylvania Compensation Rating Bureau, Risk ID 491498-1604. Large Risk Rating Plan applied. Interstate rating: No.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Insured operates as a commercial construction contractor in Pennsylvania across seven workers compensation classifications: Carpentry NOC (5403, $5,915,000 payroll), Electrical Wiring (5190, $4,544,000 payroll), Plumbing NOC (5183, $3,075,000 payroll), Masonry NOC (5022, $6,635,850 payroll), Painting Interior (5474, $1,639,000 payroll), Contractors Project Manager/Superintendent (5606, $4,776,000 payroll), and Clerical Office Employees NOC (8810, $5,010,000 payroll). Total payroll: $31,594,850.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: Actual losses over three rating years: 2023-2024 policy year — 7 claims, total paid $190,770, total incurred $191,231, primary $120,455, excess $70,776. 2022-2023 policy year — 3 claims, total paid $115,267, total incurred $116,479, primary $58,500, excess $57,979. 2021-2022 policy year — 10 claims, total paid $240,694, total incurred $240,694, primary $156,716, excess $83,978. Rating calculation inputs: Actual Primary Losses $335,671; Actual Excess Losses $212,733; Expected Losses $1,095,520; Weighting Factor 0.574; Expected Excess Losses $1,132,496; Ballast Value $13,748. Numerator $953,971; Denominator $1,109,268; Calculated Mod 0.8600.
